description_long: This object is a pastry with a golden-brown, slightly glossy exterior and a somewhat irregular, rounded rectangular shape. The surface displays a smooth, shiny finish with some cracks and folds, suggesting a soft, baked dough. The ends of the pastry are rolled inward, revealing a spiral structure typical of filled or rolled pastries. There are a few darker spots and indentations, possibly indicating areas where filling is exposed or the dough has caramelized. The bottom appears slightly lighter in color, with a more matte texture compared to the top. No visible toppings such as seeds or sugar are present on the surface.
description: A golden-brown, glossy pastry with a rolled, spiral shape and a few cracks and folds on its smooth surface.
description_view_0: The top and one side of the pastry are visible, showing a shiny, golden-brown surface with some cracks and a slightly flattened top.
description_view_1: This view highlights one end of the pastry, where the spiral roll is most prominent and a small area of filling or darker dough is exposed.
description_view_2: The opposite side is shown, with a smooth, rounded surface and less visible spiral structure, emphasizing the pastry's overall plumpness.
description_view_3: The other end of the pastry is visible, again showing the rolled spiral and a small exposed section, with a slightly more matte finish on the bottom.
